//: Visual baselines. Run against a LOCAL build: `npm run build && PORT=3220 BASE_PATH=/ npm start`,
//3: These exist because the garden's geometry cannot be unit-tested: the suite runs under happy-dom,
//4: which has no layout engine, so getBoundingClientRect returns zeros. The 14 test files assert the
//5: autotiler, the economy and placement rules — all correct, and all blind to a board with a quarter

shots.name: garden-board-mobile
shots.//: THE regression. `justify-content: center` on a scroller holding a flex:none 800px board put
shots.//2: 219px of the west corner at a negative offset, where scrollLeft cannot reach. If this shot ever
shots.//3: gets wider than its wrapper again, that bug is back.

shots.name: garden-board-desktop
shots.//: --gfit clamps to 1 past ~870px, so this must look exactly as it always has.

//7: tolerance: 1 on every shot here is deliberate, and it means these are GEOMETRY guards, not pixel guards. The garden's contents are per-user and freshly generated, so its pixels are not reproducible between runs — but its BOX is, and the box is the whole bug: a board wider than its wrapper is what stranded 219px of it. shots.mjs reports a size change before it ever counts pixels, so a size regression still fails loudly while the planting inside is free to differ. Asserting the thing that is stable beats asserting the thing that looks thorough and cries wolf.
